auth.title: Biometrics Management
auth.inactive: This device is not registered yet.
auth.loginWrong: Your Passkey and our server are currently giving each other the silent treatment. We tried to play peacemaker, but your finger/face and the system are acting like complete strangers. Try again before they start dividing their assets. P.S. Make sure your device is unlocked and ready to cooperate!

login.whatsappRead: Thoughts turned into words. The rest is out of my hands.
login.whatsappSend: Read. Now the real conversation begins – the one without words.
login.whatsappDelivered: The message is there. The question is, will you find the time?
login.whatsappDefault: Not everything can be sent digitally. Some things require presence.

nonceIdentityCrisis.title: Nonce Identity Crisis
nonceIdentityCrisis.message: It seems the nonce you provided failed the UUID test. Our server is quite old-fashioned about it and requires 36 characters of pure, hyphen-divided hexadecimal love. Please correct it before our logs are flooded with tears.

mysteriousStranger.title: Who is this mysterious stranger?
mysteriousStranger.message: It seems you are trying to use a client account that is not in our records. It might be a typo in the ID, or we just haven't met yet. Register the client in the admin panel, and we promise to greet them with open ports next time!

register.registerWrong: Registration failed. Please check your data and try again.

authInfo.title: Secure Login in Our Application
authInfo.description: Our platform offers advanced and flexible authorization methods tailored to your needs.

social.title: 1. Social Registration and Login (Social Auth)
social.description: You can register instantly using your social media account. This option allows for immediate login without the need to fill out lengthy forms.

traditional.title: 2. Traditional Registration and Verification
traditional.description: If you prefer the traditional method, provide your email address and phone number. Logging in this way requires a verification code sent to both channels every time, ensuring the highest level of data protection.

advanced.title: 3. Advanced Authentication Methods
advanced.description: Once logged in, you can further secure your account by activating modern login methods:

methods.2: Biometrics (fingerprint or facial recognition), if supported by your device

linking.title: 4. Account Linking
linking.description: Keep in mind that each login method (traditional or social) initially requires a separate registration. However, once accounts are created, they will be linked. This allows you to log into the same account in the future using any method of your choice.

sso.title: 5. Third-Party Authentication (Single Sign-On / SSO)
sso.description: Our platform acts as a trusted Identity Provider. You can now use your account to log in to external services and partner applications without the need to create new credentials for every site.
sso.dataSecurity: Data Security: Your passwords and verification codes are never shared with third-party services. Authentication occurs exclusively within our secure environment.
sso.transparency: Total Transparency: Every time you log in to an external site, you will be asked to explicitly approve the scope of data (e.g., email address, username) that will be shared with that specific application.
sso.accessManagement: Access Management: Within your security dashboard, you can monitor the list of external applications you have authorized and revoke their access at any time with a single click.

loggedInfo.title: Welcome back

settings.title: Security Settings
settings.description: In the settings tab, you can enable two-factor authentication (2FA) using an authenticator app (e.g., Microsoft Authenticator), hardware key login, or biometrics if your device supports these features.

backend.title: Backend
backend.description: The backend application was written entirely by me. If you have any feedback or suggestions, feel free to comment on GitHub!

frontend.title: Frontend (Client)
frontend.description: The client application was largely developed by Google Gemini, and I have personally customized and integrated it.

loggedInfo.loginFail: Login via social media failed. You must first register using this login method.

documentation.title: Login System Integration Documentation
documentation.subtitle: OAuth2-like Flow
documentation.intro: This documentation describes the process of implementing login in a Client-Server architecture using an external identity provider. The process is based on the secure exchange of temporary tokens and verification on the backend side.

config.title: 1. Project Configuration
config.short_desc: Learn how to register your application and get the necessary credentials.
config.desc: Before starting the implementation, you must register your application in the provider's administrative panel.

steps.1: Create a new project: Provide a unique application name.
steps.2: Define Frontend URL: Provide the exact address (e.g., https://app.yourdomain.com) to which the system will send postMessage communications.

credentials.clientId: Client ID: Public identifier of your project.
credentials.secret: Secret: Private access key (generated once). Never share it on the frontend side or in the repository source code.
credentials.loginUrl: Login URL: Entry point address for the user.
credentials.verifyUrl: Verification URL: API address for token verification on the backend side.

process.title: 2. Authentication Process Flow
process.short_desc: A step-by-step guide to the authentication flow, from initiation to token verification.

step1.title: Step 1: Initiation on the Frontend
step1.desc: To start logging in, open a popup login window under the provided Login URL.
step1.nonce: nonce: Unique session identifier in UUID format. It serves to protect against CSRF and Replay attacks.

step2.title: Step 2: Data Transfer (postMessage)
step2.desc: After successful login on the provider side, the system will send an object with data to your parent window using the window.postMessage mechanism.
step2.task: Frontend Task: Check nonce consistency and send the token to your backend.
step2.p4: | nonce | UUID sent in the previous step. |

step3.title: Step 3: Verification on the Backend
step3.desc: Your backend must confirm the authenticity of the token by communicating directly with the provider's server (Server-to-Server).
step3.p5: | token | Temporary token received from the frontend. |

step4.title: Step 4: Finalization and Session
step4.desc: If verification is successful, the provider's server will return:
step4.list: User email, JWT Token (for session authorization), Unique Session Secret (unique key signing the session).
step4.important: Recommendation: The email + session_secret pair should be saved in a fast data store (e.g., Redis) to maintain the session. The JWT token should be sent back to the frontend as confirmation of login.

step5.title: Step 5: Refresh Token Flow
step5.desc: When the short-lived accessToken (JWT) expires, the application should use the saved refreshToken (UUID) to obtain a new session without interrupting the user's work and requiring a new login through the popup.
step5.p5: | token | Refresh token received during token verification after login. |
step5.backend_code_description: Example implementation of the endpoint on the backend side (Node.js with Express).
step5.frontend_code_description: Example of handling automatic token refresh on the frontend side using an axios interceptor.

security.title: Security Summary
security.short_desc: Key security principles to follow during implementation.
security.p1: Backend-only Secret: Never send client_secret from the browser.
security.p2: Nonce Validation: Always compare the UUID sent with the one returned in postMessage.
security.p3: Token Expiration: Temporary tokens are one-time use and have a short time-to-live (TTL).

code_blocks.open_login_comment_1: // We generate a unique session identifier (Nonce)
code_blocks.open_login_comment_2: // We build the URL according to your specification
code_blocks.listener_comment_2: // Change to the actual URL of your provider
code_blocks.listener_comment_4: // Only events of type 'AUTH_SUCCESS' are processed
code_blocks.listener_comment_5: // We extract the data sent by the provider
code_blocks.listener_comment_6: // C. Nonce verification (protection against CSRF attacks)
code_blocks.listener_error_nonce: Error: Nonce mismatch! The session may have been hijacked.
code_blocks.listener_success_token: Received a valid token, sending to backend for verification...
code_blocks.listener_comment_7: // 3. Sending the temporary token to your backend
code_blocks.listener_comment_8: // We clear the nonce after use (one-time)
code_blocks.listener_comment_9: // Finalizing the login (e.g., saving JWT in localStorage or Cookie)
code_blocks.express_comment_3: // Sending a request to the Provider (Server-to-Server)
code_blocks.express_comment_5: // We return the JWT to the front-end
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_1: // We assume you have an initialized redis client
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_3: // The client sends the refreshToken (UUID) they received upon login
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_5: // 1. We search in Redis for data assigned to this UUID
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_6: // The key in Redis should be in the format e.g., 'refresh:UUID'
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_7: Session has expired or the token is invalid
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_8: // We decode the data (e.g., email or user ID) that we saved at login
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_9: // 2. Here you generate a new Access Token (JWT)
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_10: // In a real system, you would use the 'jsonwebtoken' library (jwt.sign)
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_13: // 3. Optional: Refresh Token Rotation (you also generate a new Refresh Token)
code_blocks.refresh_token_backend_comment_14: // If you don't rotate, you just send back the same one or only the new Access Token
code_blocks.refresh_token_frontend_comment_1: // Extending your axiosInstance with automatic refresh
code_blocks.refresh_token_frontend_comment_2: // If the server returns 401 (Unauthorized) and it's not a retry
code_blocks.refresh_token_frontend_comment_3: // Attempt to get a new token
code_blocks.refresh_token_frontend_comment_4: // We save the new Access Token
code_blocks.refresh_token_frontend_comment_5: // We update the header and retry the original request
code_blocks.refresh_token_frontend_comment_6: // If the refresh token has expired in Redis (after 7 days) -> full logout

qrCode.title: Fast action, zero passwords.
qrCode.description: Scan the code with your phone to log in securely using a Passkey. Already have a paired device? Just confirm the notification on your smartphone and you're in – without typing a single character!
qrCode.canceled: Change of plans? Login canceled. Let us know when you're ready to get back in the game. And now you may choice different method of login
qrCode.success: Hackers are crying – you’re in with zero effort. Let’s go!
qrCode.error: Your password and our system aren't on speaking terms. Please try again.
qrCode.alert: Intruder alert! Someone is trying to steal your spotlight (and your access).

qrCodeLogin.title: The keys to the kingdom, in your hand.
qrCodeLogin.description: Confirm your identity (FaceID, fingerprint, or PIN) to securely unlock access on the other device. It’s the safest way to tell us: 'Yes, it’s really me!'.
